title: santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. semidentata
intro: Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. semidentata, also known as grey cotton lavender, is a shrubby perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. Its leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of essential oils, such as camphor and borneol, which have been shown to have health benefits including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. semidentata can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
